GitHub
● Materials · Custom Nodes · Shadertoy Porting

Material Custom Node Reference

Engine: UE 5.x
Context: Material Custom Node (body only)
Target: SM5 / SM6 / ES3.1
Context & Setup
Always Declare Context First
WHERECustom node / .usf file / Niagara / Plugin shader
UE VERSION5.1 / 5.2 / 5.3 / 5.4+
SHADER MDLSM5 / SM6 / ES3.1
RETURN TYPEfloat / float3 / float4 / etc.
PLATFORMPC / Mobile / Console / Vulkan
Declare Input Pins Explicitly

Custom node pins become variable names. Always list them:

// Tell Claude exactly:
Inputs:
  float3  WorldNormal
  float2  UV
  float   Time
  float   Roughness

Return type: float3
Specify UE Globals Needed
→View.GameTime — elapsed time
→ResolvedView.WorldCameraOrigin
→Primitive.LocalToWorld
→View.ScreenPositionScaleBias
!If not needed, ask for self-contained code with no injected globals
GLSL → HLSL Intrinsic Mapping
Always Use HLSL Intrinsics · Full Conversion Table
GLSL (source)HLSL / UE EquivalentNotes
mix(a, b, t)lerp(a, b, t)Most common mistake from Shadertoy ports
fract(x)frac(x)Identical behavior, different name
mod(x, y)fmod(x, y)Sign behavior differs for negatives — use glsl_mod(x,y) = x - y*floor(x/y) for exact GLSL match
atan(y, x)atan2(y, x)Argument order is the same
dFdx / dFdyddx / ddyPixel shader only — not available in vertex or compute
inversesqrt(x)rsqrt(x)Fast reciprocal square root
vec2 / vec3 / vec4float2 / float3 / float4UE also accepts half2/3/4 for mobile perf
mat3 / mat4float3x3 / float4x4Row-major in HLSL vs column-major in GLSL — may need transpose
texture(sampler, uv)Texture2DSample(...)Use UE material texture input pins instead
layout(location=0) out[Implicit in Custom node]No output declarations needed in body-only mode
gl_FragCoordSvPositionIn full shaders; in Custom node use a ScreenPosition pin
step(edge, x)step(edge, x)Same name — but GLSL arg order is reversed from some docs
smoothstep(e0, e1, x)smoothstep(e0, e1, x)Same name and arg order — hermite interpolation
clamp(x, 0.0, 1.0)saturate(x)Preferred UE shorthand for 0–1 clamping; clamp() also works
pow(base, exp)pow(abs(base), exp)HLSL pow is undefined for negative base — always wrap with abs()
abs(x)abs(x)Same — but integer abs() may behave differently on some platforms
sign(x)sign(x)Same name — returns -1, 0, or 1
noise()[USE UE NOISE NODE]Not available in HLSL — use hash functions: frac(sin(dot(p, float2(12.9898, 78.233))) * 43758.5453)
textureLod(s, uv, lod)Texture2DSampleLevel(Tex, TexSampler, uv, lod)Requires Tex + TexSampler pair from input pins
textureSize(sampler, 0)[Use a float2 TexelSize input pin]No direct equivalent — pass dimensions as a parameter
Custom Node Rules
Custom Node · Never Do This
FalseNo #include — body is injected mid-shader, includes break compilation
FalseNo top-level function declarations — inline all logic or use the struct workaround
FalseNo sampler declarations — pass textures as input pins instead
FalseNo global-scope mutable state — all vars are local to the body
FalseNo GLSL precision qualifiers (mediump, highp, lowp)
FalseNo void main() — body only, return value is the output
FalseNo undeclared variable references — only use explicitly listed input pins
Custom Node · Best Practices
✓Inline-only logic — no separate function blocks
✓static const for all constants
✓Explicit return value matching declared return type
✓Swizzle count validation — float3 output ≠ float4 return
✓Step-by-step comments on each major operation
✓Feature level guard hints if targeting ES3.1 / mobile
✓Flag texture samples as [NEEDS TEXTURE PIN] rather than inline
Struct Workaround for Helper Functions

Custom nodes inject code inside a function body — top-level function defs cause C2059. Wrap helpers in a struct:

// Struct trick — works inside Custom node body
struct Helpers {
  float hash21(float2 p) {
    p = frac(p * float2(123.34, 456.21));
    p += dot(p, p + 45.32);
    return frac(p.x * p.y);
  }
  float glsl_mod(float x, float y) {
    return x - y * floor(x / y);
  }
};
Helpers H;
return H.hash21(UV);
Shadertoy / GLSL Port Guide
Shadertoy Uniform Mapping
ShadertoyUE5 EquivalentNotes
iTimefloat Time input pinConnect a Time node to the pin
iResolution.xyfloat2 Resolution input pinUse SceneTexture:SceneColor → Size or pass manually
iMouse[No direct equivalent]Pass via Material Parameter Collection or Blueprint
iFrame[No direct equivalent]Approximate with floor(Time * FrameRate)
iChannel0–3Texture Object input pinsOne pin per channel; use Texture2DSample()
fragCoordfloat2 ScreenPos input pinConnect ScreenPosition node (viewport UV)
fragColorreturn float4(...);The Custom node return value is the output
Coordinate System Pitfalls
FalseY-axis flip — Shadertoy is Y-up (0 at bottom); UE UVs are Y-down (0 at top). Apply uv.y = 1.0 - uv.y
FalseUV range — Shadertoy fragCoord is in pixels; UE ScreenPosition is 0–1 normalized
FalseAspect ratio — Shadertoy divides by iResolution.y; in UE pass resolution and do the same
FalseNDC differences — Shadertoy clip space is -1 to 1; UE uses 0 to 1 in many contexts
!Depth — Shadertoy has no depth buffer access; UE can sample SceneDepth via pin
Multi-Pass & Buffer Limitations
FalseBuffer A/B/C/D cannot be ported to a single Custom node — each buffer is a separate render pass
!Feedback loops (reading own output) require Render Targets + Blueprint tick logic
!Persistent state — use Material Parameter Collections or render-target ping-pong
✓Single-pass shaders (Image tab only, no buffers) port cleanly to one Custom node
✓Workaround — split multi-pass into separate materials, chain via render targets in Blueprint
World Position Offset / Vertex Shader
WPO Basics
✓WPO return type is always float3 — a world-space offset added to each vertex position
✓Connect Custom node output to the World Position Offset pin on the material
✓Use absolute world position input pin for world-space vertex position
✓Return float3(0,0,0) for no displacement — the value is additive
✓Great for: wind, waves, procedural animation, terrain deformation
Available Vertex Data
AbsoluteWorldPositionVertex position in world space
VertexNormalWSWorld-space vertex normal
VertexTangentWSWorld-space vertex tangent
TexCoord[0-7]UV channels available via input pins
VertexColorPer-vertex RGBA (baked in mesh)
ActorPositionObject pivot in world space
ObjectBoundsBounding box extent — useful for normalizing offsets
WPO Limitations
FalseNo ddx / ddy in vertex stage — these are pixel shader derivatives
FalseNo texture sampling by default in VS — enable Allow Custom Node Vertex Interpolators
!Shadow & bounds mismatch — large WPO can push verts outside the bounding box, causing culling. Increase Bounds Scale
!VS → PS data — to pass custom data from vertex to pixel, use Vertex Interpolator node in the material graph
!Performance — WPO runs per vertex every frame; keep math simple on high-poly meshes
Common Errors to Prevent
Gotchas That Break Compilation or Produce Wrong Results
FalseMatrix multiply order — HLSL is row-vector × matrix; GLSL is column-major
FalseUV origin — UE flips V; ask Claude to account for 1.0 - UV.y if porting
FalseSwizzle mismatch — always verify component counts explicitly
FalseShadertoy time is iTime — must be mapped to a float Time input pin
FalseInteger division — 1/2 = 0; cast to float explicitly: 1.0/2.0
!Scalar broadcast — float3(1) is valid HLSL but prefer float3(1,1,1) for clarity; some older compiler versions warn
FalseHLSL fmod sign differs from GLSL mod — use x - y*floor(x/y) for exact match
Falsepow() with negative base is undefined in HLSL — always use pow(abs(x), n)
Prompt Templates
Template A · Material Custom Node
Context: UE5 Material Custom Node (body only, no signature) UE Version: 5.4 Shader Model: SM5 Platform: PC / Vulkan Return type: float3 Input pins: float3 WorldNormal float2 UV float Time float Roughness Goal: [describe effect in plain language] Rules: - No #include, no function declarations - static const for all constants - HLSL intrinsics only (no GLSL) - Validate all swizzle component counts - Comment each major step - Return type must match: float3
Template B · Shadertoy / GLSL Port
Task: Port the following GLSL to a UE5 Custom node Source: [paste GLSL shader here] Apply ALL conversions: mix → lerp fract → frac mod → x - y*floor(x/y) // NOT fmod (sign differs) atan → atan2 dFdx/y → ddx/ddy vec2/3/4 → float2/3/4 mat3/4 → float3x3/float4x4 iTime → Time (float input pin) iResolution → ScreenSize (float2 input pin) gl_FragCoord → use a ScreenPos input pin Output rules: - Body-only, no function signature - No sampler declarations - Flag texture samples as [NEEDS TEXTURE PIN] - Use struct workaround if helper functions needed - Add a comment for each conversion made
UE5 Shader Conversion Cheatsheet · GitHub Home · Niagara · Shaders MIT License · Community Edition
